rulings.Case Title: DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS VS. LEGION CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Pleadings And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerks Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS's Motion To Amend Pleadings And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerks Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc. Non-Party State Labor Commissioner's Motion To Amend Pleadings And To Correct Clerical Error In Clerks Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Good cause appearing, the judgment may be amended nunc pro tunc to March 30, 2020, to reflect the true name of Plaintiff and judgment creditor as Tejendra Pandey. The caption may be corrected to reflect Plaintiff's true name. The court will not "amend" "all pleadings in the case" or order amendment of all pleadings to correct the name. Such relief is not feasible and not appropriate. Moving Party must present a proposed amended judgment (with caption corrected) to the Clerk of the court, along with this order, in order to secure a new judgment. Moving Party is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on April 15 2025. Moving Party shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CORINTHIA CRISTOFANI VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2025 Line 8. PLAINTIFF CORINTHIA CRISTOFANI's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two. Plaintiff Corinthia Cristofani's motion to compel further production of documents is granted. The RFPs at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant American Honda Motors Co., Inc.'s responses are incomplete. Defendant has not demonstrated that any of its objections has substantial merit. Defendant, for example, presents no competent evidence regarding burden. Plaintiff's "other vehicle" requests regarding the same alleged defects is proper. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].) Defendant must produce all-nonprivileged documents in response to requests 1 through 35 by May 7, 2025. If there are privileged documents, a privilege log should be produced. The record does not show Defendant acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery. Defendant shall pay $1,000 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by May 15, 2025, receipt filed no later than May 22, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH PELLERITI VS. AQUARIUM OF THE BAY, A NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2025 Line 4. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH PELLERITI's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions. Plaintiff Joseph Pelleriti's unopposed Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED. The motion is granted as to Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Nos. 25, 26 and 32. The requests are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (Code of Civil Procedure Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant's' responses are evasive/incomplete. Defendants have not demonstrated that any objection has merit. Plaintiffs' meet and confer was reasonable. The court orders Defendant to provide further verified production no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. Documents for which Defendant is asserting a privilege must be identified in a Code compliant privilege log. Further responses, verification (if not yet provided) and privilege log due at the same time. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiff failed to request sanctions (let alone name the party from whom they were sought or the amount sought) in the text of the notice of motion as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.040. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE AXION GROUP DBA THE ROYAL OAK VS. AVI COMMERCIAL INSURANCE MARKETING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, April 15, 2025 Line 9. PLAINTIFF THE AXION GROUP DBA THE ROYAL OAK's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Plaintiff's counsel Frederic M. Douglas's unopposed Motion to be Relieved as Counsel is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Notice is inadequate per Code of Civil Procedure section 1008 and related rules and statutes. No Proof of service on file. Further, counsel's declaration fails to articulate any basis for a court order authorizing counsel's withdraw without client consent. A conclusory statement that his "withdrawal is mandatory" is not sufficient. Moving counsel must lodge a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on April 15, 2025. Counsel shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.
